title: alchemilla hybrida subsp. glaucescens
intro: Alchemilla hybrida subsp. glaucescens, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its pale yellow flowers and silver-green foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Alchemilla hybrida subsp. glaucescens include reducing menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding, as well as treating digestive issues and wounds. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to calm and heal irritated tissues.
